title: Charge A Low-Battery Robot Until It Can Start And Rejoin System Operation
summary: Restore enough battery charge to a low-battery robot so it can start again and be returned to system operation. The source describes a manual charging approach using a reserved/disabled charger, approximate short-duration charging guidance, and re-entry once the robot has enough charge to boot and cycle on its own.
when_to_use: Use when a robot is too low on battery to continue normal operation and needs enough charge to start again so it can be added back into the system and continue charging through normal operation.
not_for: Do not use as a guaranteed battery recovery timing procedure; the source describes charging times and battery percentages as approximate training guidance.
Do not use when an exact re-entry method is required; this packet does not specify the exact steps for putting the robot back into the system.
Do not use to infer unsupported safety controls, shutdown requirements, or lockout requirements not stated in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Move or arrange movement of the low-battery robot to a charger using the source-described manual charging approach, monitor charging progress, and return the robot to the system once it can start again.

safety_notes: The source states the charger used for manual charging can be disabled so no other robot is sent there while the desired AGV is pushed into the charging station.
The source indicates charger capacity should be restored after use by turning the charger back on or re-enabling it after recovery.
Battery timing and percentage examples are approximate training guidance, not guaranteed outcomes.
The packet does not provide a detailed physical movement safety method for pushing or handling the robot; use only site-approved practices.
access_or_tools_needed: Ability to place the robot on charge
Access needed to return the robot to the system

access_required: Access to charger selection or equivalent ability to reserve/disable a charger for manual charging, if used
Access needed to return the robot to the system
system_preconditions: A charger is available for the low-battery robot
The robot is low on battery and cannot continue operating normally or cannot be turned on until taken to a charger

visual_references.description: Training frame describing manual charging with a disabled charger, low-battery thresholds, and approximate recovery timing.
Charger Selection view showing charger status and the ability to disable a charger so another AGV is not sent there during manual charging.

steps.title: Move the low-battery robot to a reserved charger
steps.purpose: Get the low-battery or dead robot onto a charger without another robot being assigned to that charging location.
steps.instruction: Use the source-described manual charging approach to reserve a charger if needed, then move the desired low-battery robot to that charging station. The source states a charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent there, and the desired AGV can then be pushed into the disabled charging station.
steps.expected_result: The low-battery robot is positioned on a charger reserved for manual charging.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger is reserved/disabled for the recovery and the intended robot is on the charging station.
steps.failure_condition: Another robot is still being assigned to the charger, the intended robot is not on charge, or the robot cannot be moved onto the charger.

actions.label: Reserve charger for manual charging
actions.exact_action: Disable the charger so no other robot is sent to that charging station.

actions.label: Place robot on charger
actions.exact_action: Push the desired AGV into the disabled charging station for manual charging.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection view showing charger ID, charger status, enabled/disabled state, and which robot is on the charger.
Slide text stating a charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent there and the desired AGV can be pushed into the disabled charging station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot cannot be moved onto the charger.
The charger cannot be reserved/disabled for manual charging.
Another robot continues to be assigned to the charger despite the attempted reservation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text: if a charger is needed to manually charge a robot, the charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent there; the desired AGV can then be pushed into the disabled charging station.

steps.title: Charge the robot until it can start again
steps.purpose: Allow the robot to regain enough battery to boot.
steps.instruction: Leave the robot on charge and wait for it to regain enough battery to start again. The source indicates the robot may boot automatically once it has enough charge.
steps.expected_result: The robot regains enough charge to start.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot reaches a state where it can start or boots automatically after charging.
steps.failure_condition: The robot remains unable to start after a reasonable charging period based on the source's approximate guidance.

actions.label: Leave robot charging
actions.exact_action: Place the robot on charge and wait for it to regain enough battery to start again.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported guidance that the robot may need to be taken to a charger and may boot automatically once it has enough charge.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not boot up after charging.
The robot does not appear to regain enough charge to start.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It only takes maybe 10 minutes to get enough charge to be able to start, add it back in the system.
Transcript mentions uncertainty about whether the robot must be on to charge manually and that it may boot automatically once it has enough charge.

steps.title: Use approximate charging guidance to judge readiness
steps.purpose: Provide source-supported expectations for short charging recovery without treating them as guaranteed thresholds.
steps.instruction: Use the training guidance only as a rough expectation: the source says it may take about 10 minutes to get enough charge to start, and gives an example that charging from about 25% for 15 minutes may bring the robot to about 40%, which is described as good enough for the system to cycle it on its own.
steps.expected_result: The operator has a rough, source-supported expectation for when the robot may be ready to start and re-enter service.
steps.healthy_condition: Charging progress is interpreted as approximate guidance only, not as a guaranteed threshold.
steps.failure_condition: Charging expectations are treated as exact requirements or the robot still cannot start or cycle after the approximate recovery window.

actions.label: Apply approximate timing guidance
actions.exact_action: Use about 10 minutes as a rough expectation for enough charge to start again.

actions.label: Apply approximate percentage example
actions.exact_action: Use the example that about 25% charged for 15 minutes may reach about 40%, which is described as good enough for the system to cycle the robot on its own.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Battery threshold and recovery discussion showing approximate timing and percentage examples for low-battery recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not start after the approximate charging period.
The robot starts but still cannot be cycled by the system after charging.
There is uncertainty requiring a more exact threshold than the source provides.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It only takes maybe 10 minutes to get enough charge to be able to start, add it back in the system.
If you're 25%, you charge for 15 minutes, you're probably gonna be at 40%, which is good enough for the system to cycle it on its own.

steps.title: Return the robot to the system after it can boot
steps.purpose: Put the recovered robot back into normal system operation so it can continue charging and operating through standard system behavior.
steps.instruction: Once the robot has enough charge to boot up, add it back into the system so it can continue charging through normal system operation. If a charger was disabled for manual charging, re-enable it after recovery so queued AGVs can again be assigned there.
steps.expected_result: The robot is returned to the system and charger capacity is restored.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is back in the system and the manually reserved charger is returned to normal service.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot be added back into the system or the charger remains unavailable after recovery.

actions.label: Add robot back into system
actions.exact_action: Add it back in the system once it has enough charge to start.

actions.label: Restore charger availability
actions.exact_action: Turn the charger back on or re-enable it after use so queued AGVs can again be assigned there.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection view used to confirm the charger can be re-enabled after manual charging.
Recovery discussion stating the robot can be added back into the system after enough charge is regained.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not boot up when attempting re-entry.
The robot cannot be added back into the system.
The charger cannot be returned to service after manual charging.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It only takes maybe 10 minutes to get enough charge to be able to start, add it back in the system.
Operators should turn the charger back on or re-enable it after use so limited charger capacity is not reduced.

steps.title: Verify the robot can cycle on its own
steps.purpose: Confirm the recovered robot has enough charge for normal system-controlled movement and charging behavior.
steps.instruction: Verify that after re-entry the system can cycle the robot on its own.
steps.expected_result: The robot continues operating and charging normally under system control.
steps.healthy_condition: The system cycles the robot on its own after re-entry.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot cycle on its own after re-entry or does not remain in normal operation.

actions.label: Confirm autonomous cycling
actions.exact_action: Verify the robot can cycle on its own after re-entry.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Approximate guidance that around 40% is good enough for the system to cycle the robot on its own.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot cannot cycle on its own after re-entry.
The robot rejoins the system but does not continue normal operation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you're 25%, you charge for 15 minutes, you're probably gonna be at 40%, which is good enough for the system to cycle it on its own.

success_criteria: The low-battery robot regains enough charge to start again.
The robot is added back into the system.
The system can cycle the robot on its own after re-entry.
If a charger was reserved/disabled for manual charging, it is re-enabled after recovery.
healthy_conditions: The robot can start after a short charging period.
The robot re-enters the system successfully.
The system cycles the robot on its own after re-entry.
Charger availability is restored after manual charging.
failure_conditions: The robot does not boot up after charging.
The robot cannot be added back into the system after charging.
The robot cannot cycle on its own after re-entry.
The charger remains disabled or unavailable after the recovery attempt.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the robot does not boot up after charging.
Escalate if the robot cannot rejoin the system after charging.
Escalate if charger reservation or re-enablement cannot be completed.
Escalate if more exact battery thresholds or re-entry steps are required than the source provides.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual charging guidance, low-battery thresholds, and approximate recovery timing.
Charger Selection screen for disabling and re-enabling a charger during manual charging.

missing_details: The exact method for putting the robot back into the system is not specified in this packet.
The source does not provide a precise required battery threshold for re-entry; only approximate examples are given.
The source does not provide a formal estimated completion time for the full procedure, only approximate charging examples.
The packet does not specify exact HMI button names or a detailed confirmation method for successful re-entry.
